teamObjective.explanation: My hand is entirely low non-trump clubs, so I cannot realistically win this trick. By leading the lowest card I invite my partner to take control with a stronger card.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner has 3 cards and no public plays yet; with my own hand weak, it is plausible partner holds at least one card above a club 3 or a trump to win the trick.

opponentBelief.summary: One opponent has only 2 cards and is close to finishing, so they may hold a high card or trump to try to win quickly. The other opponent has 3 cards and is also unknown.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Leading the club 3 minimizes the value I commit, makes it cheapest for my partner to overtake, and forces opponents to reveal or spend higher cards if they want the lead.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: The 6 is the highest card in my weak hand; playing it now wastes my only slightly better exit card without improving winning chances.
The 5 is still higher than necessary for an opening lead and leaves me with a weaker remaining shape compared to leading the 3.
The 4 is only marginally better than the 3; when trying to surrender the lead cheaply, the absolute lowest card is preferable.

risks: An opponent with a high card or trump can immediately win the trick and continue their attack.
Partner may be unable to beat even a low lead, giving the opponent with 2 cards a chance to finish.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Playing the lowest possible card reduces the risk that partner needs to spend a valuable card to overtake and keeps my own higher low cards for later exits.

notes: Opening lead with the weakest card is consistent with standard Guandan signaling when holding no trump and no high cards.
